subject: Bernie's in McConnell's backyard
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
Bernie Sanders is traveling to red states to make it clear how much is at stake with Trump and the GOP calling the shots, and why we need to fight back.
---
[****](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/resistance-fund?refcode=DPE-20170420-Battleground_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")
Bernie is on the offense, friend.
[](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/resistance-fund?refcode=DPE-20170420-Battleground_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")
He's traveling to red states while the Senate is on recess to make it clear how much is at stake with Trump and the GOP calling the shots, and why we need to fight back.

Bernie even brought that fight to Mitch McConnell's backyard.

Thousands gathered in Kentucky to make it clear that we're not backing down on our progressive values -- that we won't stop fighting for an economy that works for everyone, and for a future where families don't need to worry about how they'll pay for healthcare, send their kids to college, or put food on the table.

**Together with our Democratic Senators, we 're fighting for the middle class and leading the charge against Trump and the GOP -- that's why we're investing in ads to put the GOP on defense.**

[**Chip in $1 to our Recess Resistance Fund now to support Democratic Senators who are going to the mat every day for what we believe in.**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/resistance-fund?refcode=DPE-20170420-Battleground_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

Across the country, progressive activists have come together to speak out against every one of Trump's far-right policies that are a direct attack on American families.

And that's why we founded our Recess Resistance Fund -- to show our senators that in the fight against the flood of GOP extremism, we're right there with them.

**Are you in? Stand with Senator Sanders and the DSCC today by chipping in to our Recess Resistance Fund:**

[**http://dscc.org/resistance-recess**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/resistance-fund?refcode=DPE-20170420-Battleground_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

Thank you,
Team DSCC

**Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee
120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of the DSCC's grassroots network. Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We also strive to keep you updated on issues related to the Democratic Party's values.

If you'd like to take a break from DSCC emails, [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e19e/2b07114c/5fe94564/736c5701/1113280776/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/take-an-email-break?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e19e/2b07114c/5fe94564/736c5700/1113280776/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/supportdscc?refcode=DPE-disclaimer"). Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. If you'd like to stop receiving all emails from the DSCC, please [click here](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e19e/2b07114c/5fe94564/736c5703/1113280776/VEsB/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/content/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). body:
Everything White House for 4/21/2017. |

**FEATURED**
[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2a692fdf46878dd5cf5d7230450c1abd0c1b20295533f591818709da150f70767525b175507fb00db99e5b2637af4e39a5214e7afbd1bfe0)

**WHITE HOUSE MEMO**
A new economic optimism is sweeping across our country, and today President Donald J. Trump is taking action to continue this great economic revival. The President is signing three Presidential actions that will further the process of simplifying the tax code, holding Wall Street firms accountable, and opening up opportunities for small businesses and entrepreneurs.

**AFTERNOON:**
* **1:30PM:** President Trump meets with Secretary of State Rex Tillerson
* **2:45PM:** President Trump signs three financial services Presidential actions – **[Watch LIVE](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2a692fdf46878dd5dfbeaddb13f9a30e4e7d0808d5d9ab920f01e29a33243e1e341e0797844cc4334f9120cbb94e66c4f250dbed43146ebe)**
* **3:05PM:** President Trump meets with Secretary of the Treasury Steve Mnuchin
* **3:30PM:** President Trump meets with the Director of the Office of Management and Budget Mick Mulvaney

**FROM PRESIDENT TRUMP**
[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2a692fdf46878dd52b959307f48ecaa7a4a25051691ecddfe60492a55c163af1cfc6277cab8d3759004fe6027259aaab2d69ac75c96c0788)

body:

If you agree it’s time to... 🇺🇸 Promote American-made goods and labor → add your name today. 🇺🇸 Bring manufacturing jobs back from overseas → add your name today. 🇺🇸 MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN! → add your name today. The President has asked his campaign staff for a list of his top America First supporters, and to ensure you’re one of the first names he sees, you must submit your response by 11:59 PM TONIGHT. Show President Trump you’re ready to put America First, Friend. Add your name today if you agree it’s time to BUY and HIRE AMERICAN. Let’s bring jobs, skilled labor, and manufacturing back to the USA. Thank you for your support, Team TRUMP I SUPPORT THE USA P.S. - This email was made in the USA. I ask Republicans who want to create jobs, grow our economy, and make our Commonwealth a better place to live, work, and raise a family to join me and vote for Jill Vogel in the Republican primary on June 13th." \- Congresswoman Barbara Comstock[Congressman Bob Goodlatte also endorsed Jill](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001pX0BfzpY2rOyG3Hj3id7IfuLGSc7dTl-92i7XR7hx_pmF9ecmSi7FvYClswrM8ojW7gmyv7mdgZUoxw3rzwfIiMdFYp3DCU6HaQsMEzpdCW3iC7ZuF2x4TBQfL6Tog3UnqOMnPinCHlvzZm2Uo6ef1q1yI5yhuoa9QTgLNQrvJ3E2foQyXzWirQECQTJMmm9UDj9Pf0NbxzhcQqWl3wAzEtHwAfotYNOncBZ55qHfsBbK8mivmAtno6HTGAlfTX-_Kjn2BCFJ9ZM3SsTyTmcKA==&c=_WIu27ABGGQfLmCmy0QSbVrtCPXzKfbT0rj6_Cy2Prjfinx2y_UG4w==&ch=xdSi5_mK6wEzmxdwv2dbIGp02vrVK66NLjCJE24avuBg_3racpc93w==). name: Tom Perez
subject: Let's prove Republicans wrong again!
body:
The GOP is coming for our health care again
---
**Add your name to help Democrats defeat the GOP's health care bill -- again:

** **[ADD YOUR NAME](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Stop-the-GOP-Bill1?keycode=&firstname=\(null\)&lastname=\(null\)&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+ADD+YOUR+NAME&utm_campaign=em_20170421_tp_v1s2&source=em_20170421_tp_v1s2")**
Friend --

When we defeated Trumpcare last month, we knew the GOP wasn't done trying to take our care away.

Well, Republicans are back with a new Obamacare repeal bill. It's just as disastrous as before -- **[so we're going to need to rally together to stop it again.](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Stop-the-GOP-Bill1?keycode=&firstname=\(null\)&lastname=\(null\)&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+so+were+going+to+need+to+rally+together+&utm_campaign=em_20170421_tp_v1s2&source=em_20170421_tp_v1s2")**

This new bill -- a "compromise" between the really extreme and totally extreme wings of the GOP -- would still allow insurance companies to leave people with pre-existing conditions out in the cold. As for essential health benefits like doctor's visits and maternity care, this new plan just kicks the ball over to the states -- so if you live in a state with a Republican governor or legislature, you could be out of luck.

And when it comes to jacking up premiums for seniors and cutting $880 billion from Medicaid -- two of the main reasons the nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office projected that 24 million Americans would lose their health coverage -- nothing has changed at all.

At the end of next week, Donald Trump will mark 100 days in office without much to show for it. Republicans are desperate to give him a big, shiny trophy to wave around -- so they're talking about rushing this new health care debacle through Congress in the next few days.

Last time, the GOP thought they could jam through a disastrous repeal bill before we could stop it. We proved them wrong.

**Let's do it again. Say you're ready to stop the GOP and protect affordable care:**

**[ADD YOUR NAME](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Stop-the-GOP-Bill1?keycode=&firstname=\(null\)&lastname=\(null\)&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+ADD+YOUR+NAME&utm_campaign=em_20170421_tp_v1s2&source=em_20170421_tp_v1s2")**
Thanks -- more on this to come.

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c820/4067b0a/2f76e91f8/7e75191a/539168269/VEsC/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20170421_tp_v1s2&source=em_20170421_tp_v1s2"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c820/4067b0a/2f76e91f8/7e75191b/539168269/VEsD/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170421_tp_v1s2&source=em_20170421_tp_v1s2"). Click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c820/4067b0a/2f76e91f8/7e751918/539168269/VEsA/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170421_tp_v1s2&source=em_20170421_tp_v1s2") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c820/4067b0a/2f76e91f8/7e751919/539168269/VEsB/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=7+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170421_tp_v1s2&source=em_20170421_tp_v1s2"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

name: Basi Alonso
subject: FW: Live from your living room!
body:
Join us on April 23 for our State of the Revolution livestream
#
Hi there,

The State of the Revolution livestream is happening this Sunday, and it's going to be the spark that ignites the next political revolution. **[Don't miss out on this incredible moment -- sign up for an email reminder and let us know you'll be watching!](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0d31/43f17890/daa7dc47/6203ca87/1811114935/VEsH/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/signup/state-of-the-revolution-livestream-rsvp/?source=em170421-virtual")**
In solidarity,
Basi Alonso and the entire team at Our Revolution
\---------------------------------
Friends,
Our Revolution is thrilled to host an energizing national livestream event to kick off our summer of organizing -- and you're invited! **Join us on April 23 for our State of the Revolution livestream and help grow our movement for the next phase of the political revolution.**
In a showcase of our grassroots organizing efforts since our work officially began last August, we will present our vision to transform American politics and implement our progressive agenda. **To be a part of this, all you have to do is head to our website on Sunday, April 23 at 5 p.m. EDT.**
**[SIGN UP FOR AN EMAIL REMINDER»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0d31/43f17890/daa7dc47/6203ca87/1811114935/VEsE/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/signup/state-of-the-revolution-livestream-rsvp/?source=em170421-virtual")**

Former Ohio State Senator Nina Turner, former NAACP President Ben Jealous, former Nevada State Assemblywoman Lucy Flores, Massachusetts State Representative Mike Connolly and Our Revolution Board Chairman Larry Cohen will lead the livestream. Together, we will:
* Celebrate our victories at the local, state, and national levels
* Outline the integral role you and your community will continue to play in building our movement
* Give local leaders like you the resources and tools you need to take immediate action after the livestream

Our movement has the potential to be the largest force for progressive political action this country has ever seen -- but it will take all of us getting involved at the grassroots for it to be successful. **[Need a reminder to join us on April 23? No problem! Sign up and we'll send you a reminder when it's time to log on to the livestream.](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0d31/43f17890/daa7dc47/6203ca84/1811114935/VEsF/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/signup/state-of-the-revolution-livestream-rsvp/?source=em170421-virtuall")**
Our political revolution relies on people like you. Your dedication makes our work possible.
In solidarity,
The Team at Our Revolution

WASHINGTON, DC, 20002

Donations to Our Revolution are not deductible as charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes. All donations are made to support Our Revolution's general mission and are not designated for any specific activity.
603 2nd St NE, Washington, DC 20002

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: in support of science
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __
The United States is a world leader in innovative research. Whether it's finding cures for diseases, understanding the impact of climate change, or building the next generation of telescopes to explore our universe, critical work is taking place around us every day here in Maryland and across the country to advance our knowledge, improve our lives, and protect the future of our planet.

Until now our federal government has played a vital role in supporting this research and providing funding for agencies that advance public health, environmental safety, and critical scientific innovation. But under the Trump administration this role is under threat.

President Trump's budget would drastically cut funding for programs that support investments in scientific and medical research and protect sensitive ecosystems like the Chesapeake Bay.

[**This weekend as we celebrate Earth Day I hope you will stand with me and my Democratic colleagues in support of science and human progress.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91be3/70cdc2ba/4755dc78/397f79b/757883510/VEsH/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/sign-add-your-name-to-say-we-must-fund-not-cut-scientific-funding-?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+This+weekend+as+we+celebrate+Earth+Day+I&utm_campaign=em042117&source=em042117")

Governing is about choices. We can pay for a massive wall along our southern border or advance scientific inquiry and innovation which opens up new frontiers, fuels economic growth, and helps us find solutions to the biggest challenges facing our planet.

[**Sign here to send a message to Trump and the Republican Congress that America must support funding for science, innovation and medical research because our future depends on it.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91be3/70cdc2ba/4755dc78/397f798/757883510/VEsE/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/sign-add-your-name-to-say-we-must-fund-not-cut-scientific-funding-?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Sign+here+to+send+a+message+to+Trump+and&utm_campaign=em042117&source=em042117")

Traveling across Maryland I am regularly inspired by the incredible work of scientists, engineers and teachers at the many top notch research institutions and agencies we have in our state. The important work of these dedicated individuals deserves to be funded.

I hope to see some of you tomorrow at the March for Science and the discussion beforehand at the Stuart Mott House. But wherever you are this Earth Day I hope you will keep up the fight!

Chris

name: New Hampshire Republican Party
subject: Your Weekly NH GOP Newsletter
body:
| |
---
|
**NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER** FRIDAY, APRIL 21st |
---|---|---
---
---
---
| | _**A Note From Jeanie**_ | |
---
| Dear Derek,
FINALLY! It feels like we are making progress when it comes to standing up for children here in New Hampshire! Governor Sununu and the Republican legislature continue to make headway—whether it's making sure that every child, no matter their zip code, has access to an education that fits their needs (HB620), or strengthening our laws to eliminate child trafficking (HB94). This week the Senate also passed HB412 that would establish pre-engineering and technology curriculum programs for New Hampshire students.
With our new Department of Education Commissioner Frank Edelblut and Governor Sununu’s recent nomination of Union Leader editorial page director Drew Cline to the Board of Education, New Hampshire students will benefit from fresh, new ideas that focus on high academic standards, local control and respect for the role parents play in their child’s education.
It is so exciting that we have a vision and a focus to make this state a better place for our children to live, learn, and grow.

CONCORD- Today, the House Election Law Committee heard testimony regarding SB 3, relative to domicile for voting purposes.
This legislation clarifies the definition of domicile for voting purposes while modifying the requirements for documenting the domicile of an individual registering to vote. SB3 will prohibit individuals who are only in the state on a temporary basis, and who maintain a voting domicile in another state, from voting here.
House Majority Leader Richard “Dick” Hinch (R-Merrimack) issued the following statement after today’s hearing:
“New Hampshire plays an important national role in elections due to our First in the Nation status and as a battleground swing state. We have numerous elections for state representative each cycle being decided by only a few votes, and statewide elections being decided by less than a 1% margin. In order to maintain confidence in the electoral process it is extremely important that every vote is cast by a duly qualified voter. SB3 seeks to eliminate drive-by voting by clearly defining domicile. It will treat every voter equally and ensures everyone who shows up to the polls has an opportunity to vote if they are qualified.
I look forward to this legislation coming out of committee with an Ought to Pass recommendation so the House can act on this bill and swiftly send it to the Governor’s desk.”

| _**Senate Republicans**_
**Innis, Sanborn pleased by passage of bill exempting virtual currency from state regulation** Concord, NH -- Today, the Senate passed a bill which would allow virtual currency, like Bitcoin, to be exempt from state regulation and would provide regulation instead through the Consumer Protection Act.
Senator Dan Innis (R-New Castle) and Senator Andy Sanborn (R-Bedford) issued the following statements:
“Due to the nature of virtual currency as an emerging industry, we need to be cautious about overregulation and potentially stifling its use or business growth in the State of New Hampshire,” said Senator Dan Innis (R-New Castle). “Allowing Bitcoin and other virtual currency to be regulated by the consumer protection act provides certainty for consumers while allowing this kind of currency to continue to be used.”
“Bitcoin and other virtual currency continues to be a safe, private form of real currency which is an appealing alternative for many peer-to-peer transactions,” Senator Andy Sanborn (R-Bedford). “We need to allow innovating businesses like Bitcoin this to grow and I am pleased that the senate voted in support of this measure to refrain from over regulating this emerging industry today.” |

| **Ward applauds legislation expanding school choice** Concord, NH - Today, Senator Ruth Ward (R-Stoddard) issued the following statement upon passage of legislation that would expand school choice for students in towns without their own public schools.
“This is the right solution for this complex problem for the upcoming ’17-’18 school year and I applaud the Senate’s support of this measure which will allow greater school choice for students and parents,” said Senator Ruth Ward (R-Stoddard).
“In communities like the town of Croydon, which I represent, that does not have its own public schools, it is essential that students have the choice of where they attend school to best suit their individual educational needs.”
“This bill expands options for New Hampshire students by allowing private schools that are in the process of earning full accreditation from the New England Association of Schools and Colleges to be among their choices,” added Ward. |

| **Reagan pleased by passage of bill expanding pre-engineering, technology curriculum for NH students** Concord, NH – The Senate today passed a bill, HB 412, which would establish a pre-engineering and technology curriculum for public school students in grades K-12.
“I am pleased to have the support of the Senate today in passing legislation that will expand pre-engineering and technology programs for New Hampshire students,” said Senator John Reagan (R-Deerfield).
“We heard convincing testimony revealing that for students to become interested in Science, Technology, Engineering and Math subjects [STEM], they must be introduced to these topics starting as early as kindergarten which is why this legislation expands the pre-engineering curriculum from grades 6-12 to include all students in grades K-12.”
“New Hampshire is also facing a skilled worker shortage. With the growing high-tech industry jobs, it is more important than ever that we provide the resources and education needed to give New Hampshire students the skills that will help them to fill good jobs like these in our state,” said Reagan. |

---
|
Gerrie Porter was born in Caribou, Maine in 1932, the daughter of Cecil and Violet Burgess. In 1960, she, her husband, and son moved to New Hampshire from Turkey and finally settled in Amherst in 1965.

Gerrie was a District Representative to U.S. Representatives James Cleveland, Judd Gregg, and Charles Douglas. An active member of the Republican Party, she served as President of the Amherst Republican Women's Club, President of the New Hampshire Federation of the Republican Women, and was on the Board of the Hillsborough County Republican Committee. She also attended a number of National Republican conventions as a delegate or alternate.

Gerrie was a dedicated Republican and friend and will be missed dearly. |

clean_body: | | | NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, APRIL 21st | ---|---|--- | | A Note From Jeanie | | | Dear Derek, FINALLY! It feels like we are making progress when it comes to standing up for children here in New Hampshire! Governor Sununu and the Republican legislature continue to make headway—whether it's making sure that every child, no matter their zip code, has access to an education that fits their needs (HB620), or strengthening our laws to eliminate child trafficking (HB94). This week the Senate also passed HB412 that would establish pre-engineering and technology curriculum programs for New Hampshire students. With our new Department of Education Commissioner Frank Edelblut and Governor Sununu’s recent nomination of Union Leader editorial page director Drew Cline to the Board of Education, New Hampshire students will benefit from fresh, new ideas that focus on high academic standards, local control and respect for the role parents play in their child’s education. It is so exciting that we have a vision and a focus to make this state a better place for our children to live, learn, and grow. Speaking of learning and growing, if you haven’t already, please be sure to on Saturday, April 29th at the NH Institute of Politics. This is an opportunity to hone your social media skills. Governor Sununu and Senate President Chuck Morse will be attending to share brief remarks. Sincerely, Jeanie | | | Join the Grassroots Circle! | | The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party. Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. There are three levels of support: The Grassroots Supporter, the NHGOP Advocate and the VIP Investor. | | | | Intern with the NH GOP! | | Are you a student or know a student that is interested in Politics? Learn the skills necessary to win elections and meet the Republican leaders that govern our State and write legislation. | | | Upcoming Events | ---|---|--- | NH College Republican Annual Convention April 22 - 10:00 AM Wedu Conference Room, 20 Market Street, Manchester, NH NH Young Republicans - April Social April 25 - 7:00 PM Murphy's Taproom, 494 Elm Street, Manchester, NH Cheshire County GOP Lincoln Day Dinner w/ Special Guest Gov. Chris Sununu April 28 - 6:00 PM Keene Country Club, 755 West Hill Rd, Keene, NH NHGOP Grassroots CommunicationsSummit April 29th - 8:30 AM St. Anselms College - Institute of Politics, 100 St. Anselm Drive, Manchester, NH For more information Prayers for Prevention - A grassroots movement to help prevent suicide & spread awareness May 7 Statewide For more information, NHGOP "Spring to Victory" Dinner with Special Guest Kellyanne Conway! May 18 - 6:00 PM Radisson Hotel, 11 Tara Blvd, Nashua, NH To RSVP & for more information | | | | | | Event Highlight | ---|---|--- | Sullivan County GOP Gala with Special Guest Nick Adams! | | Join the Sullivan County Republican Committee on May 19th for an evening gala at the beautiful Sunapee Lodge at the Mt. Sunapee Ski area with special guest speaker, Nick Adams of . The event is Semi Formal (Gentlemen, please wear a dinner jacket) VIP opportunity available We promise a fun evening including a book signing by Nick Adams, a silent auction with a variety of items and packages for all ages, along with a few more special guests! | ---|---|--- | | Photo of the Week | ---|---|--- Jodi Nelson, Vice Chair of the Derry GOP, was one of several Granite Stater's invited to the White House this week for the New England Patriots visit and reception after their win at this year's Super Bowl! | Republican Spotlight! | ---|---|--- | Thank you for all of your submissions! This week's spotlight shines on the Republican State Representative, Kimberly Rice. She was nominated by NH State Rep. Yvonne Dean-Bailey & Hudson GOP Chair Michael Tranfaglia. Representative Rice is in her second term and continues to work hard as the Chair of the Children and Family Law Committee and on the Joint Committee Overview of DCYF and the DCYF Advisory Board. We are grateful for all of Kim's hard work and continued efforts to protect the children and families of New Hampshire! Who do you think should be in the spotlight in next week's newsletter? | | | Legislative Highlights | ---|---|--- | House Republicans Majority Leader Hinch Urges Support of SB3 in House Election Law Committee CONCORD- Today, the House Election Law Committee heard testimony regarding SB 3, relative to domicile for voting purposes. This legislation clarifies the definition of domicile for voting purposes while modifying the requirements for documenting the domicile of an individual registering to vote. SB3 will prohibit individuals who are only in the state on a temporary basis, and who maintain a voting domicile in another state, from voting here. House Majority Leader Richard “Dick” Hinch (R-Merrimack) issued the following statement after today’s hearing: “New Hampshire plays an important national role in elections due to our First in the Nation status and as a battleground swing state. We have numerous elections for state representative each cycle being decided by only a few votes, and statewide elections being decided by less than a 1% margin. In order to maintain confidence in the electoral process it is extremely important that every vote is cast by a duly qualified voter. SB3 seeks to eliminate drive-by voting by clearly defining domicile. It will treat every voter equally and ensures everyone who shows up to the polls has an opportunity to vote if they are qualified. I look forward to this legislation coming out of committee with an Ought to Pass recommendation so the House can act on this bill and swiftly send it to the Governor’s desk.” | | Senate Republicans Innis, Sanborn pleased by passage of bill exempting virtual currency from state regulation Concord, NH -- Today, the Senate passed a bill which would allow virtual currency, like Bitcoin, to be exempt from state regulation and would provide regulation instead through the Consumer Protection Act. Senator Dan Innis (R-New Castle) and Senator Andy Sanborn (R-Bedford) issued the following statements: “Due to the nature of virtual currency as an emerging industry, we need to be cautious about overregulation and potentially stifling its use or business growth in the State of New Hampshire,” said Senator Dan Innis (R-New Castle). “Allowing Bitcoin and other virtual currency to be regulated by the consumer protection act provides certainty for consumers while allowing this kind of currency to continue to be used.” “Bitcoin and other virtual currency continues to be a safe, private form of real currency which is an appealing alternative for many peer-to-peer transactions,” Senator Andy Sanborn (R-Bedford). “We need to allow innovating businesses like Bitcoin this to grow and I am pleased that the senate voted in support of this measure to refrain from over regulating this emerging industry today.” | | Ward applauds legislation expanding school choice Concord, NH - Today, Senator Ruth Ward (R-Stoddard) issued the following statement upon passage of legislation that would expand school choice for students in towns without their own public schools. “This is the right solution for this complex problem for the upcoming ’17-’18 school year and I applaud the Senate’s support of this measure which will allow greater school choice for students and parents,” said Senator Ruth Ward (R-Stoddard). “In communities like the town of Croydon, which I represent, that does not have its own public schools, it is essential that students have the choice of where they attend school to best suit their individual educational needs.” “This bill expands options for New Hampshire students by allowing private schools that are in the process of earning full accreditation from the New England Association of Schools and Colleges to be among their choices,” added Ward. | | Reagan pleased by passage of bill expanding pre-engineering, technology curriculum for NH students Concord, NH – The Senate today passed a bill, HB 412, which would establish a pre-engineering and technology curriculum for public school students in grades K-12. “I am pleased to have the support of the Senate today in passing legislation that will expand pre-engineering and technology programs for New Hampshire students,” said Senator John Reagan (R-Deerfield). “We heard convincing testimony revealing that for students to become interested in Science, Technology, Engineering and Math subjects [STEM], they must be introduced to these topics starting as early as kindergarten which is why this legislation expands the pre-engineering curriculum from grades 6-12 to include all students in grades K-12.” “New Hampshire is also facing a skilled worker shortage. With the growing high-tech industry jobs, it is more important than ever that we provide the resources and education needed to give New Hampshire students the skills that will help them to fill good jobs like these in our state,” said Reagan. | | NHGOP Press Releases | ---|---|--- | April 20 | | Congrats to Senator Scott Brown! | ---|---|--- | Congratulations to Senator Scott Brown on his nomination to be the U.S. Ambassador to New Zealand! | | In Memoriam | ---|---|--- | | Gerrie Porter was born in Caribou, Maine in 1932, the daughter of Cecil and Violet Burgess. In 1960, she, her husband, and son moved to New Hampshire from Turkey and finally settled in Amherst in 1965. Gerrie was a District Representative to U.S. Representatives James Cleveland, Judd Gregg, and Charles Douglas. An active member of the Republican Party, she served as President of the Amherst Republican Women's Club, President of the New Hampshire Federation of the Republican Women, and was on the Board of the Hillsborough County Republican Committee. She also attended a number of National Republican conventions as a delegate or alternate. Gerrie was a dedicated Republican and friend and will be missed dearly. | | | This Week's Best Tweets! | ---|---|--- | Tweet With Us! | | ---|---|--- | Connect with your Republican Leaders! | ---|---|--- | | | | | Speaker of the House Shawn Jasper | | | Senate President Chuck Morse | | | Executive Council | ---|---|--- | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | ---|---|--- | | Website "FOLLOW" on Twitter "LIKE" on Facebook | ---|---|--- | | | | Governor Chris Sununu | | | Chairman Forrester & The NH GOP | ---|---|--- | | Web - "LIKE" on Facebook \- "FOLLOW" on Twitter - | | | Web - Facebook \- Twitter - Chairman Jeanie Forrester "LIKE" on Facebook - "FOLLOW" on Twitter - "FOLLOW" The Staff on Twitter Finance Director - Political Director - | | 10 Water Street, Concord, NH 03301 Phone: (603) 225-9341 | Email: info@nhgop.org | | | ---|---|--- | | mailto:? | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com Republican Party of New Hampshire · 10 Water Street · Concord · New Hampshire · 03301 \--UNSUBSCRIBEALT--

body:
Our Revolution groups and supporters nationwide are gathering to host State of the Revolution
#
Friends,
Join activists and supporters from every corner of the state at Our Revolution Maryland's first statewide meeting.
**[Please click here to RSVP to the statewide Our Revolution Maryland meeting](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0d33/43f17890/da880c6d/6203c944/3736077866/VEsH/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/event/detail/townhall/gpghsz")**

**Sunday, April 23 3:30 - 4:30 PM**
Tommy Douglas Conference Center
10000 New Hampshire Ave
Silver Spring, MD 20903
**During the meeting, you'll:**

* Hear reports from leaders in all Maryland counties
* Get Our Revolution Maryland's assessment of the 2017 Maryland legislative session
* Discuss next steps for Maryland

Immediately following the meeting, join Senator Nina Turner, former NAACP President Ben Jealous, former Nevada State Assemblywoman Lucy Flores, Massachusetts State Representative Mike Connolly and Our Revolution Board Chairman Larry Cohen for the live recording of the State of the Revolution livestream.
**[Please click here to RSVP to the statewide Our Revolution Maryland meeting](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0d33/43f17890/da880c6d/6203c944/3736077866/VEsE/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/event/detail/townhall/gpghsz")**
Solidarity,
OR Maryland
\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: OurRevolution.com
Date: Thu, Apr 13, 2017 at 8:42 PM
Subject: Join us for the filming of the State of the Revolution livestream, Live in Silver Spring!
Sisters and brothers,
Our Revolution groups and supporters nationwide are gathering to host State of the Revolution livestream events to celebrate how far we have come and outline the journey ahead. The political revolution is just beginning, and communities like yours will be on the frontlines of the fight for the progressive agenda that has brought us together, and **Our Revolution Maryland, a state-wide organizing committee, is excited to announce that we're hosting the event!**
**On Sunday, April 23, join local supporters for the live filming of the State of the Revolution livestream event in Silver Spring. Be a part of the mass people-powered movement to transform American politics.**
**Tommy Douglas Conference Center**
Sunday, April 23 4:30 - 6:00 PM
10000 New Hampshire Ave
Silver Spring, MD 20903
**[RSVP TO ATTEND»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0d33/43f17890/da880c6d/6203c945/3736077866/VEsF/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/event/detail/townhall/gpghsk")**

The livestream will be headlined by former Ohio State Senator Nina Turner, former NAACP President and CEO Ben Jealous, former Nevada State Assemblywoman Lucy Flores, Massachusetts State Representative Mike Connolly and Our Revolution Board Chairman Larry Cohen.

Together, we'll:
* Celebrate the victories you and supporters like you have made possible both locally and nationally
* Outline the integral role you and your community will play in continuing the movement
* Introduce the tools, resources, and campaigns you and your group can use to take immediate action after the livestream

Our movement has the potential to be the largest force for progressive political action this country has ever seen -- but it will take all of us getting involved at the grassroots for it to be successful. **Will you join the live filming of the State of the Revolution livestream on April 23 with progressives in Silver Spring?**
_" You are standing up and fighting back, and that is what the political revolution is about." \- Bernie Sanders_
In solidarity,
Our Revolution Maryland

clean_body: Our Revolution groups and supporters nationwide are gathering to host State of the Revolution Friends, Join activists and supporters from every corner of the state at Our Revolution Maryland's first statewide meeting. "" Sunday, April 23 3:30 - 4:30 PM Tommy Douglas Conference Center 10000 New Hampshire Ave Silver Spring, MD 20903 During the meeting, you'll: Hear reports from leaders in all Maryland counties Get Our Revolution Maryland's assessment of the 2017 Maryland legislative session Discuss next steps for Maryland Immediately following the meeting, join Senator Nina Turner, former NAACP President Ben Jealous, former Nevada State Assemblywoman Lucy Flores, Massachusetts State Representative Mike Connolly and Our Revolution Board Chairman Larry Cohen for the live recording of the State of the Revolution livestream. "" Solidarity, OR Maryland \---------- Forwarded message ---------- From: OurRevolution.com Date: Thu, Apr 13, 2017 at 8:42 PM Subject: Join us for the filming of the State of the Revolution livestream, Live in Silver Spring! Sisters and brothers, Our Revolution groups and supporters nationwide are gathering to host State of the Revolution livestream events to celebrate how far we have come and outline the journey ahead. The political revolution is just beginning, and communities like yours will be on the frontlines of the fight for the progressive agenda that has brought us together, and Our Revolution Maryland, a state-wide organizing committee, is excited to announce that we're hosting the event! On Sunday, April 23, join local supporters for the live filming of the State of the Revolution livestream event in Silver Spring. Be a part of the mass people-powered movement to transform American politics. Tommy Douglas Conference Center Sunday, April 23 4:30 - 6:00 PM 10000 New Hampshire Ave Silver Spring, MD 20903 "" The livestream will be headlined by former Ohio State Senator Nina Turner, former NAACP President and CEO Ben Jealous, former Nevada State Assemblywoman Lucy Flores, Massachusetts State Representative Mike Connolly and Our Revolution Board Chairman Larry Cohen. Together, we'll: Celebrate the victories you and supporters like you have made possible both locally and nationally Outline the integral role you and your community will play in continuing the movement Introduce the tools, resources, and campaigns you and your group can use to take immediate action after the livestream Our movement has the potential to be the largest force for progressive political action this country has ever seen -- but it will take all of us getting involved at the grassroots for it to be successful. Will you join the live filming of the State of the Revolution livestream on April 23 with progressives in Silver Spring?
